more.title: Abdeslam Ouaddou rubbishes Orlando Pirates exit rumours after European links
more.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has quashed speculation about his future, reaffirming his commitment to the club after a successful season that saw them secure a domestic treble.

more.title: SASSA pensioners call for R5 000 old age grant
more.description: More than 200 pensioners gathered outside the SASSA offices in Beacon Valley, Mitchells Plain, on Monday, calling on government to raise the Older Person’s Grant from R2 400 to R5 000 a month, reports Cape {town} Etc. According to TygerBurger the demonstration formed part of an ongoing campaign by Pensioners of South Africa (Posa), which has been picketing SASSA offices... Read more → The post SASSA pensioners call for R5 000 old age grant appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: Chery's investment in South Africa: Local production of Tiggo 4 Cross set for 2027
more.description: Chery South Africa has announced plans to begin local production of the Tiggo 4 Cross in 2027, following the acquisition of a manufacturing plant from Nissan. This strategic move highlights the growing influence of Chinese automotive brands in South Africa's market. (85%)

more.title: Toyota’s multi-billion-rand vote of confidence in SA
more.description: But it still wants policy change – it won’t be able to increase the level of local content in the new Toyota Hilux, among other things, until that happens.

more.title: Rand under pressure as investors monitor latest US strikes on Iran
more.description: The South African rand came under pressure on Thursday, as the latest escalation in hostilities between the United States and Iran pushed oil prices back up and reignited inflation concerns.• At 08:35, the rand traded at 16.35 against the dollar, near its previous close as investors held back from making big bets.• The US struck Iran’s coastal defences and missile sites on Wednesday after reimposing a naval blockade of its ports, while Iran threatened to shut off more regional energy exports, saying it was engaged in an “existential war” with America.• The US dollar last...

more.title: What is ‘Know Your Customer’ in the betting industry in South Africa?
more.description: Know Your Customer’ (KYC) is a mandatory process in South Africa’s betting industry that protects players, operators, and the integrity of the game. Closely tied to FICA regulations, KYC helps verify identities and addresses while preventing fraud and money laundering. Required Documents for KYC Verification in South Africa Most betting sites ask for two main [...] The post What is ‘Know Your Customer’ in the betting industry in South Africa? appeared first on Sports-Focus.co.za .

more.title: Toyota nears completion of its $634 million South Africa investment as new Hilux pickup enters production
more.description: Toyota South Africa Motors (TSAM) has completed more than 77% of its 10.4 billion rand ($634 million) investment programme to manufacture the next-generation Hilux pickup truck, reinforcing the country's position as one of Toyota's most important production hubs outside Japan.

more.title: Should the PIC be placed under administration?
more.description: ‘No, of course not. For an entity with R3.6trn in assets, the idea that one person could have unilateral authority is untenable. The solution is to find the right governance model’ – independent analyst Khaya Sithole.

more.title: SA rugby trailblazer Aimee Barrett-Theron temporarily steps back for breast cancer treatment
more.description: Aimee Barrett-Theron, South Africa's first female rugby referee, announces break from officiating to focus on her health following a breast cancer diagnosis.

more.title: Illegal Immigrants Repatriation Efforts Continue at Musina Centre as Home Affairs Processes Over 55,000
more.description: MUSINA, Limpopo — The repatriation of illegal immigrants at the temporary centre in Musina continues to see fluctuating daily numbers, according to the Department of Home Affairs, as the government programme progresses into its second month of operation.
